title: urochloa trichopus
intro: Urochloa trichopus, also known as Ririo, is a medicinal plant species native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands, including Samoa and Tonga. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as relief from fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as wound healing and skin care. Urochloa trichopus is prepared in various herbal forms, including decoctions, infusions, and poultices, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
